findings.issue: Task TDD-Anchor list names a NEW file `DefaultAppSettingsRepositoryTest.kt`; the implementer instead appended the 3 required observe-tests (plus one bonus sentinel test) to the pre-existing `AppSettingsRepositoryTest.kt`. The two files would test the same class with identical fixtures, so consolidation is rational and avoids duplication ‚Äî but the task wording was not followed literally. Flag as an acceptable deviation, not a defect.
findings.fix: Either (a) note the deviation in decisions.md for Task 2 so future readers know why the file name diverges from the anchor spec, or (b) rename the existing file to `DefaultAppSettingsRepositoryTest.kt` to match the anchor. Preferred: (a), since the existing file already predates this task and touches other concerns.

findings.issue: `newComponents_accepted` fully re-executes 6 of the 14 component-writes already covered by `knownComponents_accepted` (which now asserts all 14 including the Phase-3 six). The redundancy adds no regression protection: if a Phase-3 component is removed from `ALLOWED_COMPONENTS`, both tests fail simultaneously. Test-quality rule: every test must catch a failure no other test catches.
findings.fix: Either delete `newComponents_accepted` and rely on `knownComponents_accepted` (preferred ‚Äî it already asserts the 14-count invariant and writes each component), or repurpose `newComponents_accepted` to a targeted negative case (e.g., assert that one of the pre-Phase-3 names like `inference` is still accepted and a typo like `engine_warmup` with underscore is rejected). The current duplication is acceptable for TDD-anchor traceability but wasteful.

findings.issue: `import org.junit.Assert.assertNull` is present but never used. Unused imports are a minor code-hygiene issue.

Asymmetry in coverage: `observeDefaultModelId_emitsEmptyString_whenUnset` exists, but there is no parallel `observeLastUsedModelId_emitsEmptyString_whenUnset`. Both flows rely on the same `.map { it.<field> }` plumbing on top of the same DataStore, so the second one is not strictly necessary for regression protection, but the asymmetry is visible. Task only required the `defaultModelId` variant, so not a blocker.
findings.fix: Optional: add a one-liner `observeLastUsedModelId_emitsEmptyString_whenUnset` for symmetry. Skip if redundancy concern outweighs completeness.

notes:
All 9 TDD-anchor tests are present (one delivered under a renamed equivalent ‚Äî tests live in `AppSettingsRepositoryTest.kt` rather than a new `DefaultAppSettingsRepositoryTest.kt`; same test class, same DataStore fixture reused). The consolidation is acceptable and should be noted in decisions.md.

Litmus test results for every meaningful test:
- `knownComponents_accepted`: removing any Phase-3 string from `ALLOWED_COMPONENTS` makes `errorLog.e(component, "ok")` throw IAE ‚Üí test fails. Passes.
- `newComponents_accepted`: same, passes litmus but is redundant with the above.
- `observeDefaultModelId_emitsEmptyString_whenUnset`: if proto default changes or `.map { it.defaultModelId }` is broken ‚Üí fails. Passes.
- `observeDefaultModelId_emitsCurrentValue` / `observeLastUsedModelId_emitsCurrentValue`: if setter is a no-op or the observe flow does not map the right field ‚Üí fails. Passes.
- `settingsMigrated_flagFalseByDefault_trueAfterMark`: if `markSettingsMigrated` is a no-op or `isSettingsMigrated` returns the wrong field ‚Üí fails. Passes.
- `happyPath_rekeysNameToModelId`: if rekey logic is removed the old key remains and the new key is absent ‚Üí two assertions fail; also sentinel assert fails. Strong.
- `orphanKey_droppedAndLogged`: reads the real `errors.log` file and counts lines containing `"OrphanName"` with component `[settings-io]`. This is NOT mock-based ‚Äî it is a genuine end-to-end assertion on the side effect. If orphan logging is removed the count becomes 0 ‚Üí fails. Passes.
- `sentinel_preventsRerun`: counts `CountingDataStore.updateCount` before and after the second invocation. Note: `markSettingsMigrated` goes through the repository into `rawDataStore`, NOT through `CountingDataStore`, so only the rekey `updateData` is counted. The assertion (countAfterFirst == updateCount) is correct for the sentinel guard. If the guard were removed, the second run would call updateData on the counting store ‚Üí fail. Passes.
- `singleUpdateDataCall_perRun`: with 3 overrides, asserts updateCount == 1 on CountingDataStore. Verifies atomicity. Strong.
- `emptyOverrides_marksSentinel_withoutLogging`: bonus test; asserts sentinel is set AND `errors.log` does not exist ‚Äî meaningful because an over-eager orphan-logging impl would create the file. Good.

Orphan-log assertion is meaningful (NOT a mock-only test): it reads the physical file produced by the real `ErrorLog` writer, uses substring matching on line content, and asserts exactly 1 matching line. This verifies real behavior end-to-end.

Single-updateData invariant is reliably verified: `CountingDataStore` is a thin wrapper that delegates `data` reads to the real DataStore and counts `updateData` calls. The migration helper is injected with the counting wrapper, while the repository (used for the sentinel) is injected with the raw delegate ‚Äî so sentinel writes do not pollute the count. The design cleanly isolates the atomic-write invariant from sentinel bookkeeping.

Test isolation:
- SettingsMigrationHelperTest uses `TemporaryFolder` for the proto file (fresh per test) and `errorLogFile.parentFile?.deleteRecursively()` in both @Before and @After for the log directory.
- Each test gets a fresh UUID-named proto file, a fresh DataStoreFactory instance, and a fresh registry.
- Robolectric shares `filesDir` across tests within a suite, so the @Before deletion is necessary and correctly implemented. Mirrors the existing `ErrorLogTest` pattern.
- No shared static state observed.

Mocking strategy: only `ModelRegistry` is a hand-rolled fake (`FakeModelRegistry`), and only the `models` StateFlow is exercised ‚Äî all other methods throw or no-op. This is well under the 3+ dependency threshold for excessive mocking. DataStore is real, repository is real, ErrorLog is real writing to real files. Strong integration-flavored unit tests, appropriate for migration coordination logic.

No tautological / false-positive assertions detected. No mock-return anti-pattern. All assertions verify observable state (DataStore contents, log file lines, updateCount, sentinel boolean).

Overall: high-quality, well-isolated tests. Findings are all cosmetic (`nit` severity) and do not block approval.
